title: 'You're expected to perform, but no one wants to know how you do it': Cocaine, the toxic fuel of young professionals
subhead: Cocaine at work (1/3). Among young professionals, the illegal and highly addictive drug is used in a relentless pursuit of performance, often shrouded in taboo and without questioning of working conditions.

subhead: Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, a 52-year-old contractor awaiting regularization of his status, was shot dead by an agent claiming to have acted in self-defense, a claim disputed by the victim's friends and family.

title: France's Socialists opt for a presidential primary, but who will run?
subhead: Ségolène Royal, the party's 2007 nominee, threw her hat in the ring. But Raphaël Glucksmann and François Hollande are among those holding off.

title: Le Monde's eight favorite exhibitions at Arles Photography Festival
subhead: Our culture critic has selected her favorite exhibitions among the 36 offerings at the major photography festival held throughout the southern French city.

title: Tiago Rodrigues, director of the Avignon Festival: 'Thanks to Europe, thanks to democracy, how lucky I have been!'
subhead: 'I wouldn't be where I am today if...' In this series, Le Monde interviews a personality about a decisive moment in their life. The director of the Avignon Festival discusses the indelible mark that the Portuguese dictatorship left on his family history.

subhead: In California, the rise of artificial intelligence no longer generates the unanimous enthusiasm it once did. Anxiety is mounting, especially among students and engineers, as jobs have been cut and democratic risks have grown more threatening.

title: How Ukraine is turning Crimea, the Russians' Riviera, into a battlefield
subhead: The annexed Ukrainian peninsula, which Vladimir Putin sees as his crowning jewel, has descended into chaos as Kyiv ramps up attacks on the territory. Shocked tourists and residents express their distress as shortages worsen.
